lucius_sulla_convo_0_n01.npcDialogue: You've been watching me all evening — no, don't apologize, it's refreshing. Everyone else here watches from behind their wine cups and thinks I don't notice. You just... looked. So I'll extend you the courtesy of directness. What is it you want from Lucius Cornelius Sulla?

options.playerDialogue: Your name beside mine. Doors open for men you've spoken well of — I'd rather not pretend otherwise.
The Mithridatic command. I want to understand what you see coming in the east — before everyone else does.
The same thing you want — to be standing when Rome finishes falling down.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n02.npcDialogue: Honest appetite. I can almost taste it. Half the men who orbit me want precisely what you want — they just dress it up in philosophy first, which wastes everyone's time. But my endorsement isn't a gift, Crispus. It's an investment. And I don't invest in anything I haven't tested.

options.playerDialogue: Then test me. What do you need done?

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n03.npcDialogue: Mithridates has spent ten years building what Rome spent ten years ignoring. Whoever takes that command will either save the Republic's eastern provinces or lose them forever — there is no middle outcome. The Senate assigned the command to me. The law is clear. But since when has clarity stopped anyone in this city?

options.playerDialogue: You think Marius will try to take it from you.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n04.npcDialogue: Oh, sit down. Please. You're the first person tonight who's said something that didn't bore me — I'd hate to lose you to the crowd.

options.playerDialogue: I had a feeling you'd prefer the version without flattery.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n05.npcDialogue: Here is the shape of things. The eastern command is mine — lawfully, demonstrably mine. Marius wants it anyway. The tribunes will help him take it. If this stops being a legal question — and it will — I need to know where a man like you plants his feet.

responses.playerDialogue: Where the law plants them. Precedent is the only thing holding this Republic together — even for you.
Wherever prevents a war between you and Marius. The only man who profits from that fight is Mithridates.
With whoever moves first and means it. Hesitation is the only defeat that matters.
With whoever holds the legions when it's over. I'd rather be useful to that man than principled under the other.

fallbackResponse.playerDialogue: I'm still calculating where Rome's interests lie. Give me time.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n06.npcDialogue: You deserve a straight answer, so here it is. Marius is a ruin with a reputation — a name men remember from a war they've already forgotten. If he reaches for what the law gave me, he will discover what it means to take something from my hand. This is not a threat. I don't make threats. I'm describing the physics of the situation.

options.playerDialogue: I think I'm beginning to understand the physics.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n07.npcDialogue: Careful. I respect careful — it's kept me alive more than once. But let me be something other than careful for a moment.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n08.npcDialogue: A legion's loyalty runs to its general — not to the Senate, not to the People, not to the city on the hill that appointed him. This is the fact that no one in Rome will say aloud. I'm saying it to you now, Crispus. I want to know if you understand what that means — not as philosophy, but as iron in the street.

options.playerDialogue: I understand exactly what you're telling me. And I'm still sitting here.
I understand it completely. Tell me how to be useful.
That is a line no Roman has ever crossed. I won't help you be the first.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n09.npcDialogue: Good. I need men inside the city — men who can read a room, carry a message, and know when not to be seen doing either. Men who can keep certain names off certain lists and put other names on them. The kind of work that history forgets but power remembers. Can you do that for me?

options.playerDialogue: I'm already doing it in my head. Ask me.
Tell me exactly what you need — I don't make promises I haven't costed.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n10.npcDialogue: Then we understand each other. I have an excellent memory, Crispus — for the men who help me and for the men who don't. You've just made that particular talent work in your favor.

lucius_sulla_convo_0_n11.npcDialogue: Hm. Cautious — or perhaps just not ready. I'll remember your face. When events accelerate — and they will — you'll have one more chance to show me which it was. Don't waste it.

operations.reason: Sulla has taken you into his confidence
Word gets out that you've met with Sulla

deltas.reason: You didn't flinch when Sulla showed you his hand

operations.reason: Sulla has noted you as potentially useful
